facts.claim: Full name: Dipendra Singh Airee. Born January 24, 2000.
Born in Gajjar, Mahendranagar, Kanchanpur, Nepal (Far-Western Province).
Role: Batting all-rounder. Batting: Right-handed. Bowling: Right-arm off-break (converted from fast bowling due to recurring injuries).
Father: Surendra Singh Airee — former Nepal national cricketer who played in the 1990s. Son was directly inspired by father's cricket career.
Mother: Naru Devi Airee. She was present at TU Ground watching Dipendra play for Sudurpashchim Royals in the NPL (2024/25 season).
Siblings: One older brother (Himal/Sandeep Airee — also a cricketer); three sisters (Asha, Ashmita, Lata).
Education: Attended Cosmopolitan English Medium School, Bhimdatta Nagar. Parents wanted him to pursue a career in the military, not cricket.
Skipped 11th grade (class 11) final board exams to accept Nepal Premier League (NPL) invitation — the decisive turning point in choosing cricket over formal education.
Employed by Nepal Police. Promoted in rank by Nepal Police in October 2023 — shortly after breaking the world T20I fastest fifty record at the Asian Games.
T20I debut: July 29, 2018 vs Netherlands at Lord's — Nepal's first-ever official T20I match. He was 18 years old.
ODI debut: August 1, 2018 vs Netherlands. Scored 33 runs.

NCA contract dispute: December 2021 — Airee, along with Gyanendra Malla, Kamal Singh Airee, and Sompal Kami, publicly expressed dissatisfaction with CAN's central contract grading. CAN ruled this violated the code of conduct. Airee was stripped of vice-captaincy (held since November 2019) and lost central contract eligibility for the first six months of 2022.

Fastest T20I fifty in history: 9 balls (52* off 10 balls) vs Mongolia, Asian Games, Hangzhou, September 27, 2023. Broke Yuvraj Singh's 16-year-old record of 12 balls (set off Stuart Broad, 2007 ICC World T20).

Six sixes in one T20I over: April 13, 2024 vs Qatar, ACC Premier Cup, Muscat/Oman. Bowler: Kamran Khan. Nepal were 174/7 when he came to the crease; scored 64 off 21 balls (28 off 15 before the final over, then 36 off 6). Only the third player in T20I history to achieve this — after Yuvraj Singh (2007) and Kieron Pollard (2021).
First Nepali to score a T20I century: 110* vs Malaysia, April 2, 2022.
First ODI century for Nepal: 105 vs Papua New Guinea, March 26, 2022.
T20I career (as of early 2026): 94 matches, 2,125 runs (avg 33.20, SR 139.53, highest 110*), 60 wickets (avg 20.17, ER 6.10, best 4/18).
ODI career (as of early 2026): 68 matches, 1,241 runs (avg 21.77, SR 74.31, highest 105), 42 wickets (avg 35.33, ER 4.03, best 3/18).
ICC Men's T20I All-Rounder Ranking: #4 in the world (as of 2026).
First Nepali player selected in PSL: Islamabad United, PSL 2026. Signed for PKR 6 million — the first Nepali in Pakistan Super League history.
2026 T20 World Cup: Nepal chased 171 vs Scotland in 19.2 overs. Airee scored a match-winning 23-ball fifty to seal Nepal's first T20 World Cup win in 12 years.

facts.claim: Franchise leagues: Chitwan Tigers (EPL 2018), Montreal Tigers (GT20 Canada 2023, champion), Gulf Giants (ILT20 UAE), Islamabad United (PSL 2026), Sudurpashchim Royals (NPL, captain 2025).

cricinfo_id discrepancy: player .md file records cricinfo_id as 1151682, but ESPNCricinfo search results return profile URL with ID 794707. HowStat uses PlayerID=4809. The correct ESPNCricinfo ID may be 794707 — Sujan should verify and correct the player file if needed.

flagged_gaps: Father Surendra Singh Airee as former Nepal national cricketer confirmed by multiple Tier 3 sources only (InstantBiography, SportsGrail) — no Tier 1/2 source cross-reference. Mark as unverified but high-confidence given consistency across sources.
Mother name 'Naru Devi Airee' confirmed only via Sudurpashchim Royals official TikTok (not Tier 1/2 press). Mark as unverified.
2026 T20 World Cup Scotland match fifty (23 balls) — confirmed by web search aggregated result only, no direct Tier 1 source URL retrieved.
cricinfo_id in player file (1151682) conflicts with ESPNCricinfo search URL (794707). Sujan should verify the correct ID before relying on live stat injection.

No verbatim, datable direct quote from Dipendra Singh Airee found from a Tier 1/2 source. Existing hero_quote in player file ('म रेकर्ड बनाउन खेल्दिनँ...') appears to be an unattributed paraphrase — should be replaced with an editorial line.
